nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
### This n8n template demonstrates how easy it is to build an Outlook Calendar Assistant powered by an AI agent equipped with Tools.

n8n's AI agents makes it easy to build powerful assistants which can interact with your existing services and tools. With little effort, you can expose such an agent to team members and colleagues though something like Slack and enable a company-wide productivity booster.

### How it works
* A Slack Trigger node is configured to catch "bot mentions" events in a designated channel.
* The message is parsed using the Edit fields node to extract only the required attributes of the event.
* An AI Agent equipped with Outlook Calendar Tools enables question and answer capability for the organisation's shared calendars and events.
* The AI agent's response is sent back to Slack as a reply to the user's query.

### How to use
* The workflow is triggered via @mention-ing the bot followed by the query. eg. "@bot how many meetings does Paul have to attend to this week?"
* To start listening to real mentions, you must activate the workflow and set it to production mode. You must use the production webhook URL for the event subscription.


### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
### Setting Up Slack App Event Subscriptions
1. Go to {{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}
2. Create or Select your App
3. Under Features, click into "Event Subscriptions"
4. On this page, toggle on the "Enable Events" option
5. Enter the production URL of this template - your workflow but be active and publicly accessible for this to work.
6. Slack will issue a "challenge" request to this workflow which will respond and verify the subscription.
7. If successful, under "subscribe to bot events", find and select the "app_mention" option.
8. Hit "save changes" at the bottom of the page.
9. This workflow should now trigger when your bot is "@mention" in the channel.

parameters.content:
### This n8n template demonstrates how easy it is to build an Outlook Calendar Assistant powered by an AI agent equipped with Tools.

n8n's AI agents makes it easy to build powerful assistants which can interact with your existing services and tools. With little effort, you can expose such an agent to team members and colleagues though something like Slack and enable a company-wide productivity booster.

### How it works
* A Slack Trigger node is configured to catch "bot mentions" events in a designated channel.
* The message is parsed using the Edit fields node to extract only the required attributes of the event.
* An AI Agent equipped with Outlook Calendar Tools enables question and answer capability for the organisation's shared calendars and events.
* The AI agent's response is sent back to Slack as a reply to the user's query.

### How to use
* The workflow is triggered via @mention-ing the bot followed by the query. eg. "@bot how many meetings does Paul have to attend to this week?"
* To start listening to real mentions, you must activate the workflow and set it to production mode. You must use the production webhook URL for the event subscription.


### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## 1. Listen for Bot Mentions
[Read more about Webhook Trigger]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

**Example**:
`@bot how many meetings does Paul have to attend to this week?`

### Setting Up Slack App Event Subscriptions
1. Go to {{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}
2. Create or Select your App
3. Under Features, click into "Event Subscriptions"
4. On this page, toggle on the "Enable Events" option
5. Enter the production URL of this template - your workflow but be active and publicly accessible for this to work.
6. Slack will issue a "challenge" request to this workflow which will respond and verify the subscription.
7. If successful, under "subscribe to bot events", find and select the "app_mention" option.
8. Hit "save changes" at the bottom of the page.
9. This workflow should now trigger when your bot is "@mention" in the channel.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
microsoft Outlook  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Locate the webhook node named "Respond to Challenge".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Locate the webhook node named "On BOT/APP Mention".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Search All Outlook Events" node (n8n-nodes-base.microsoftOutlookTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create New Calendar Event" node (n8n-nodes-base.microsoftOutlookTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Available Calendars" node (n8n-nodes-base.microsoftOutlookTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Simple Memory" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Outlook Calendar Assistant" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Reply" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Open the "Is Auth Challenge?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Get Message".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
